root_text.an4.95:2.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, chavālātaṁ ubhato padittaṁ, majjhe gūthagataṁ, neva gāme kaṭṭhatthaṁ pharati na araññe (…);
root_text.an4.95:2.2: tathūpamāhaṁ, bhikkhave, imaṁ puggalaṁ vadāmi yvāyaṁ puggalo nevattahitāya paṭipanno no parahitāya.
root_text.an4.95:3.1: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ puggalo parahitāya paṭipanno no attahitāya, ayaṁ imesaṁ dvinnaṁ puggalānaṁ abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro ca.
root_text.an4.95:3.2: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ puggalo attahitāya paṭipanno no parahitāya, ayaṁ imesaṁ tiṇṇaṁ puggalānaṁ abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro ca.
root_text.an4.95:3.3: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ puggalo attahitāya ceva paṭipanno parahitāya ca, ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ puggalānaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca pāmokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro ca.
root_text.an4.95:4.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, gavā khīraṁ, khīramhā dadhi, dadhimhā navanītaṁ, navanītamhā sappi, sappimhā sappimaṇḍo, sappimaṇḍo tattha aggamakkhāyati;
root_text.an4.95:4.2: evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ puggalo attahitāya ceva paṭipanno parahitāya ca, ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ puggalānaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca pāmokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro ca.
root_text.an4.95:4.3: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an4.95:1.1: “Mendicants, these four individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an4.95:1.3: One who practices to benefit neither themselves nor others;
translation_text.an4.95:1.4: one who practices to benefit others, but not themselves;
translation_text.an4.95:1.5: one who practices to benefit themselves, but not others; and
translation_text.an4.95:1.6: one who practices to benefit both themselves and others.
translation_text.an4.95:2.1: Suppose there was a firebrand for lighting a funeral pyre, burning at both ends, and smeared with dung in the middle. It couldn’t be used as timber either in the village or the wilderness.
translation_text.an4.95:2.2: The individual who practices to benefit neither themselves nor others is like this, I say.
translation_text.an4.95:3.1: The individual who practices to benefit others, but not themselves, is better than that.
translation_text.an4.95:3.2: The individual who practices to benefit themselves, but not others, is better than both of those.
translation_text.an4.95:3.3: But the individual who practices to benefit both themselves and others is the foremost, best, chief, highest, and finest of the four.
translation_text.an4.95:4.1: From a cow comes milk, from milk comes curds, from curds come butter, from butter comes ghee, and from ghee comes cream of ghee. And the cream of ghee is said to be the best of these.
translation_text.an4.95:4.2: In the same way, the individual who practices to benefit both themselves and others is the foremost, best, chief, highest, and finest of the four.
translation_text.an4.95:4.3: These are the four individuals found in the world.”

variant_text.an4.95:1.6: attahitāya ceva → attahitāya ca (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
